name: Beef Stroganoff with Fluffy Rice and Red Onion Parsley Pickle
description: Quick beef stroganoff recipe that's creamy, light and cooks in the time it takes to do the rice. Tender strips of beef in a tangy, creamy mushroom sauce with a fresh onion and parsley pickle.
ingredients: 1 mug (300g) of 10-minute wholegrain or basmati rice
method: Put 1 mug of rice, 2 mugs of boiling water, the thyme leaves and a pinch of sea salt and black pepper into a medium pan with lid on, stirring occasionally
Peel the onions, then finely slice them with the gherkins in a food processor and tip into a bowl
Finely slice the parsley stalks and roughly chop the leaves, then toss into the bowl with a swig of gherkin vinegar and a pinch of salt, scrunching together well
Tear or slice the mushrooms into a large frying pan with 2 tablespoons of oil, then squash in the unpeeled garlic through a garlic crusher and add two-thirds of the parsley pickle, stirring regularly
Slice the steaks into 1cm strips and season with salt, pepper, paprika and lemon zest
Transfer the mushroom mixture to a bowl, add the steak strips to the hot pan in a single layer and cook until golden
Add the brandy to the steak and carefully flambé if desired, letting the flames subside
Return the mushroom mixture to the pan, stir in the yoghurt and milk, and bring to a gentle boil
Add the spinach to a colander, place on top of the rice pan with the lid inside the colander to act as a steamer for 5 minutes
Transfer the wilted spinach to a serving platter, fluff up and scatter the rice over the top
Spoon over the stroganoff, squeeze and drain the remaining pickle, then scatter over from a height
